sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Reimburse the lost deposit for the Ikigai Info governance action, including compensation for alleged lost staking rewards.
Recipient: Ikigai Info governance action submitter
Deliverables: Instant on-chain distribution of funds to the intended recipient upon enactment
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 103,000 ADA from the treasury to reimburse a 100,000 ADA governance action deposit plus 3,000 ADA for staking rewards.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): The proposer states that an Info governance action titled "Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -" was submitted in September 2024 shortly after the Chang hard fork.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): The proposer states that the original Ikigai action was symbolic and asked the community whether they agreed with a statement thanking contributors and expressing hope for Cardano's future.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposer states that a Cardano node bug allowed an unregistered stake key to be used in the governance action, preventing the submitter from recovering the 100,000 ADA deposit.
Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): The proposal references an external governance record on adastat.net related to the Ikigai governance action.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposer states that there was significant community sentiment at the time that the Ikigai deposit should be reimbursed.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposer states that a Cardano node bug allowed an unregistered stake key to be used in the governance action, preventing the submitter from recovering the 100,000 ADA deposit.
Independent evidence for: The proposer states that there was significant community sentiment at the time that the Ikigai deposit should be reimbursed.
Independent evidence for: The proposer states that Cardano in Oceania included this reimbursement in a budget Info governance action, but that governance action was not approved.
Independent evidence for: The proposer states that the reimbursement recipient has not previously received treasury funds.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 103,000 ADA from the treasury to reimburse a 100,000 ADA governance action deposit plus 3,000 ADA for staking rewards." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposer states that a Cardano node bug allowed an unregistered stake key to be used in the governance action, preventing the submitter from recovering the 100,000 ADA deposit." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
